passages.title: Corporate Social Responsibility
passages.source_html:
Corporate Social Responsibility


Acting in a socially responsible way can give companies a competitive advantage as well as benefiting the community


A


In the 1980s, Norwegian Prime Minister Gro Harlem Brundtland gave the following definition of corporate social responsibility (CSR): 'Meeting the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs.' Nowadays, governments, activists and the media are quick to hold companies to account for the social consequences of their actions. As a result, corporate social responsibility has become a priority for business leaders around the world. When a well-run business applies its vast resources and expertise to social problems that it understands and in which it has a stake, it can have a greater impact than any other organization. Although many companies have already done much to improve the social and environmental impact of their activities, these efforts are not always as productive as they could be.


B


To advance CSR we need first to acknowledge the interrelationship between a corporation and society. Successful corporations need a healthy society. Education, health care and equal opportunities are essential to a productive workforce. Safe products and working conditions not only attract customers but also lower internal costs as there are fewer accidents, while good government and strong regulatory standards protect both consumers and competing companies from exploitation. Ultimately, a healthy society creates demand for business as more human needs are met and aspirations grow. At the same time, a healthy society needs successful companies to create the jobs, wealth and innovation that improve social conditions over time.


C


A company's impact on society also changes over time, as social standards evolve and science progresses. Asbestos, now understood to be a serious health risk, was thought to be safe in the early 1900s, given the scientific knowledge then available. Evidence of its risks gradually mounted for more than 50 years before any company was held liable for the harm it can cause. Many firms that failed to anticipate the consequences of this evolving body of research have been bankrupted by the results. No longer can companies be content to monitor only the obvious social impacts of today. Without a careful process to identify evolving social effects, firms may risk their very survival.


D


However, no business can solve all of society's problems or bear the cost of doing so. Instead, each company must choose issues that intersect with its particular business. Other social agendas are best left to companies in other industries or government institutions that are better positioned to address them. The essential test that should guide CSR is not whether a cause is worthy but whether it presents an opportunity to create shared value-that is, a meaningful benefit for society that is also valuable for the business. Each company should try to identify the social problems that it is best equipped to help resolve. Addressing social issues in this way will lead to self-sustaining solutions that are not dependent on government subsidies.


E


The best corporate citizenship initiatives involve far more than simply donating money; they specify clear goals and measure their effectiveness over time. A good example is the manufacturing company GE's program to adopt public high schools in poorer areas near several of its major US facilities. The company contributes between $250,000 and $1 million over a five-year period to each school. GE's managers and employees take an active role by working with school administrators to assess needs and tutor students. GE conducted a study of ten schools in the program between 1989 and 1999 and nearly all showed significant improvement in their academic standards. Programs such as this one create goodwill and improve relations with local governments. Their effect is inherently limited, however. No matter how beneficial the program is, it remains incidental to the company's manufacturing business, and the direct effect on GE's recruiting and retention is modest.


F


Software company Microsoft's partnership with the American Association of Community Colleges is a good example of a mutually beneficial opportunity. The shortage of information technology workers is a significant constraint on Microsoft's growth; currently there are 450,000 unfilled IT positions in the US alone. Community colleges with an enrollment of 11.6 million students, representing 45% of all US undergraduates, could provide a solution. Microsoft recognizes, however, that these colleges face special challenges: IT curricula are not standardized, technology used in classrooms is often outdated, and there are no professional development programs to keep teachers up to date. Microsoft's $50 million, five-year initiative was aimed at all three problems. In addition to contributing money and products, Microsoft sent employee volunteers to colleges to assess needs and contribute to curriculum development. Microsoft's program benefited many communities.


G


A final example is the company Whole Foods Market. Its aim is to sell organic, healthy food to customers who are passionate about food and the environment. The company sources all of its products from local farmers and screens out up to 100 common ingredients that it considers to be unhealthy or environmentally damaging. Furthermore, Whole Foods' stores are constructed using a majority of recycled materials and the company recently bought renewable wind energy credits equal to 100% of its electricity use. Any spoiled produce is trucked to regional centers for composting and all of their vehicles are being converted to biofuels. In this initiative, even the cleaning products used in their stores are environmentally friendly. In short, the entire company's operations reinforce its ideals, helping to distinguish Whole Foods from its competitors.

groups.title: Questions 1-13
groups.source_html:
Questions 1-7


The text has seven paragraphs. Choose the correct heading for each paragraph and move it into the gap.

Options (Word Bank):
A. How CSR may help one business to expand
B. CSR in every aspect of a company's business
C. A CSR initiative without direct financial gain
D. Lack of action by the state in social issues
E. Current pressures forcing companies to address CSR
F. The past shows us businesses are responsible for future outcomes
G. Companies should be selective in their choice of CSR
H. The reason business and society depend on each other
1
正确答案： 1 E. Current pressures forcing companies to address CSR  |  2 H. The reason business and society depend on each other  |  3 F. The past shows us businesses are responsible for future outcomes  |  4 G. Companies should be selective in their choice of CSR  |  5 C. A CSR initiative without direct financial gain  |  6 A. How CSR may help one business to expand  |  7 B. CSR in every aspect of a company's business
Question 1

解析：

A段主旨句为“Nowadays, governments, activists and the media are quick to hold companies to account for the social consequences of their actions.”原文提到政府、活动家和媒体要求企业对社会后果负责，迫使企业履行企业社会责任。

Question 2

解析：

B段主旨句为“Successful corporations need a healthy society.”原文提出成功的企业需要一个健康的社会。

Question 3

解析：

C段主旨句为“A company's impact on society also changes over time, as social standards evolve and science progresses. Asbestos, now understood to be a serious health risk, was thought to be safe in the early 1900s, given the scientific knowledge then available.”原文提到石棉的例子，表明不考虑未来的社会影响会对公司造成伤害。

Question 4

解析：

D段主旨句为“However, no business can solve all of society's problems or bear the cost of doing so. Instead, each company must choose issues that intersect with its particular business.”原文提出每家公司都必须选择与其特定业务相关的问题。

Question 5

解析：

E段主旨句为“The best corporate citizenship initiatives involve far more than simply donating money; they specify clear goals and measure their effectiveness over time. A good example is the manufacturing company GE's program to adopt public high schools in poorer areas near several of its major US facilities.”原文提出最好的企业公民倡议不仅仅是简单的捐款，GE计划在其美国几家主要工厂附近的贫困地区开设公立高中

Question 6

解析：

F段主旨句为“The shortage of information technology workers is a significant constraint on Microsoft's growth; currently there are 450,000 unfilled IT positions in the US alone. Community colleges with an enrollment of 11.6 million students, representing 45% of all US undergraduates, could provide a solution.”原文提出如何通过CSR(与社区大学合作）解决社会需求(缺乏IT工作者)，为公司的发展创造更大的人才库。

Question 7

解析：

G段主旨句为“In short, the entire company's operations reinforce its ideals, helping to distinguish Whole Foods from its competitors.”原文提出Whole Foods是一个例子，他们对健康和可持续食品的承诺体现在从采购到废物处理的所有实践中。

Questions 8 and 9


Complete the summary. Write ONE WORD ONLY in each gap.

8

How to advance CSR

First we need to accept the interrelationship between business and society. Productivity increases if employees are healthy, educated and given .

正确答案： 8 opportunities
Question 8

定位关键词：

business, society


原文：

B段，“Education, health care and equal opportunities are essential to a productive workforce.”


解析：

原文提到教育、保健和平等机会对有生产力的劳动力至关重要。

9

Restrictions imposed by government can stop both businesses and consumers from being treated unfairly. Improved conditions reduce the number of in the workplace. Similarly, society benefits as businesses develop.

正确答案： 9 accidents
Question 9

定位关键词：

businesses, consumers, workplace


原文：

B段，“Safe products and working conditions not only attract customers but also lower internal costs as there are fewer accidents, while good government and strong regulatory standards protect both consumers and competing companies from exploitation.”


解析：

原文提到安全的产品和工作条件可以减少事故，降低公司的内部成本。

Questions 10-13


Choose the correct company (A – C) for each statement. You may choose any correct company more than once.


List of Companies

A GE

B Microsoft

C Whole Foods Market


Question / ParagraphABC
10

10 the disposal of waste
✅ 正确答案：C

定位关键词：

Whole Foods Market


原文：

G段，“Any spoiled produce is trucked to regional centers for composting and all of their vehicles are being converted to biofuels.”


解析：

原文提Whole Foods Market到将变质的农产品制成堆肥，并使用环保清洁产品。
11

11 the way a company purchases its goods
✅ 正确答案：C

定位关键词：

Whole Foods Market


原文：

G段，“The company sources all of its products from local farmers”


解析：

原文提到Whole Foods Market的所有产品都来自当地农民。
12

12 helping the underprivileged
✅ 正确答案：A

定位关键词：

 GE


原文：

E段，“A good example is the manufacturing company GE's program to adopt public high schools in poorer areas near several of its major US facilities.”


解析：

原文提到GE计划在其美国几家主要工厂附近的贫困地区开设公立高中。
13

13 ensuring educators have the latest information
✅ 正确答案：B

定位关键词：

Microsoft


原文：

F段，“Microsoft recognizes, however, that these colleges face special challenges: IT curricula are not standardized, technology used in classrooms is often outdated, and there are no professional development programs to keep teachers up to date. Microsoft’s $50 million, five-year initiative was aimed at all three problems.”


解析：

原文提出微软帮助社区大学开发IT课程，并使教师与时俱进。
